nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for novel tasks
nodes.label_negative: Traditional manual coding for novel tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would have needed to write a new piece of complex code from scratch. This is why I turned to AI as it helped to speed up the process and work more efficiently.

nodes.label: AI as inspiration and phrasing aid
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous writer without human oversight
nodes.grounding_span: I use it for inspiration and ideas, but I read all output carefully and only use something if I agree with it.

nodes.label: AI reliability in scientific reasoning
nodes.label_negative: AI failure in scientific reasoning and interpretation
nodes.grounding_span: It often seems to fail in its scientific reasoning leading to interpretations and discussion points that range from missing key points to being downright wrong.

nodes.label: AI transparency and reproducibility
nodes.label_negative: Hidden biases and unseen algorithms in AI
nodes.grounding_span: hidden biases and reproducibility (unseen algorithms/pipelines, etc). These are two hurdles that currently prevent me from feeling reassured enough to use AI more than I currently do.

nodes.label: AI use in core established workflows
nodes.label_negative: AI use in core established workflows
nodes.grounding_span: for some core parts of my work, I already have access to an appropriate software suite and catalogue of models and there is no requirement to use AI

nodes.label: Research efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: helped to speed up the process and work more efficiently

nodes.label: Scientific accuracy and integrity
nodes.grounding_span: I always check these carefully by hand as I think this is an area where mistakes are more likely to slip through

nodes.label: Scientific understanding and interpretation
nodes.grounding_span: I wish I could provide AI with the output of some analysis and, given the context, have it interpret it in an accurate and reliable manner

nodes.label: Positive stance toward AI for efficiency in novel tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I turned to AI as it helped to speed up the process and work more efficiently

nodes.label: Cautious stance toward AI for writing and summarization
nodes.grounding_span: I use it for inspiration and ideas, but I read all output carefully and only use something if I agree with it

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI for scientific reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: It often seems to fail in its scientific reasoning leading to interpretations and discussion points that range from missing key points to being downright wrong

nodes.label: Negative stance toward AI due to hidden biases and reproducibility concerns
nodes.grounding_span: hidden biases and reproducibility (unseen algorithms/pipelines, etc). These are two hurdles that currently prevent me from feeling reassured enough to use AI more than I currently do

nodes.label: Verification-oriented approach — careful checking of AI outputs
nodes.grounding_span: I read all output carefully and only use something if I agree with it

nodes.label: Domain-specific trust calibration — reliance on existing validated tools
nodes.grounding_span: for some core parts of my work, I already have access to an appropriate software suite and catalogue of models and there is no requirement to use AI
